data.title: Aliko Dangote Advances Succession Plan by Elevating Daughters' Roles
data.description: Africa's wealthiest individual, Aliko Dangote, is advancing his succession strategy by assigning more significant responsibilities to his daughters within his business empire. Bloomberg posted on X that this move is seen as a crucial step in ensuring the continuity of leadership at Dangote's conglomerate. The decision reflects Dangote's commitment to preparing the next generation for leadership roles, ensuring the sustainability and growth of his business interests.

data.title: Strategy's average BTC holding cost has decreased for the first time in 2.5 years.
data.description: According to Arkham monitoring, Michael Saylor's average BTC purchase price has decreased. Strategy's purchase of BTC for $168.4 million lowered MSTR's average cost base by $29 to $76,027. This is the first decline in this figure in nearly two and a half years since September 25, 2023.

data.title: Lombard Odier Faces Lawsuit from New York Portfolio Managers
data.description: Two portfolio managers from Lombard Odier in New York have filed a lawsuit against the Swiss financial firm. Bloomberg posted on X, alleging that the company isolated their ESG private credit fund and deprived it of necessary investment. The managers claim that this action has hindered the fund's growth and performance. The lawsuit highlights tensions within the firm regarding the management and support of ESG-focused financial products. Lombard Odier has yet to comment on the allegations. The case underscores the challenges faced by financial institutions in balancing traditional investment strategies with emerging ESG priorities.

data.title: Glencore Secures Agreement with Democratic Republic of Congo to Boost Copper Production
data.description:
Glencore has finalized an agreement with the Democratic Republic of Congo (DRC) to acquire a land package aimed at enhancing its copper production capabilities. Bloomberg posted on X that this deal is expected to significantly increase Glencore's copper output, aligning with the company's strategic goals to expand its mining operations in the region.

The agreement involves the acquisition of land that will allow Glencore to access additional copper reserves, thereby bolstering its production capacity. This move is part of Glencore's broader strategy to capitalize on the growing demand for copper, a key component in various industries, including electronics and renewable energy.

The Democratic Republic of Congo, known for its rich mineral resources, plays a crucial role in the global copper market. Glencore's expansion in the DRC is anticipated to strengthen its position as a leading player in the mining sector.

This development comes at a time when the global demand for copper is on the rise, driven by technological advancements and the transition to greener energy solutions. Glencore's increased production capacity is expected to contribute to meeting this demand, providing a boost to both the company and the DRC's economy.

data.title: Gabon Suspends Social Media Amid Protests Over Living Costs
data.description: Gabon has suspended all social media platforms indefinitely, according to the country's media regulator. Bloomberg posted on X that this decision comes as the government confronts increasing protests driven by the rising cost of living. The suspension aims to curb the spread of information that could exacerbate the situation. The move has sparked concerns over freedom of expression and access to information in the nation. The government has not provided a timeline for when the suspension might be lifted.

data.title: GEOPOLITICS | India and Serbia Discuss Strengthening Bilateral Cooperation
data.description: Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi met with Serbian President during the sidelines of the 'Artificial Intelligence Impact Summit' today. According to Jin10, the leaders explored ways to enhance bilateral cooperation in trade and investment, artificial intelligence, fintech, education, agriculture, tourism, and cultural exchanges. They also exchanged views on regional and global issues of mutual concern.

data.title: Henry Boot Announces Job Cuts Amid Property Sector Downturn
data.description: Henry Boot, a prominent UK developer, is reducing its workforce following a downturn in the property sector. Bloomberg posted on X that this decision comes shortly after the company sold its construction business, resulting in a 21% reduction in its workforce. The move reflects the challenges faced by the property industry, impacting employment within the company.

data.title: STOCKS | European Markets Open Higher Amid Positive Sentiment
data.description: European stock indices opened higher on February 18, reflecting a positive market sentiment. According to Jin10, the UK's FTSE 100 index rose by 33.28 points, or 0.32%, to 10,589.45 points. France's CAC 40 index increased by 8.06 points, or 0.10%, reaching 8,369.52 points. The Euro Stoxx 50 index climbed 19.90 points, or 0.33%, to 6,041.75 points. Spain's IBEX 35 index saw an uptick of 81.01 points, or 0.45%, to 18,036.41 points. Italy's FTSE MIB index gained 244.43 points, or 0.53%, reaching 46,008.50 points. Meanwhile, Germany's DAX 30 index rose by 109.93 points, or 0.44%, to 25,115.27 points. The upward movement across these indices indicates a generally optimistic outlook among investors in the European markets.

data.title: Scammer Drains $22,000 From Bank of America Account, Pushing Customer To Conduct Fake ‘Sting Operation’
data.description:
A Bank of America customer in Massachusetts just lost tens of thousands of dollars to an elaborate bank scam. Police say the victim was contacted by someone posing as a Bank of America fraud investigator, reports the ABC-affiliated news station WCVB.
source: https://dailyhodl.com/2026/02/18/scammer-drains-22000-from-bank-of-america-account-pushing-customer-to-conduct-fake-sting-operation/

data.title: South African Inflation Nears Central Bank's Target in January
data.description: South Africa's inflation rate decreased in January, moving closer to the central bank's target of 3%. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting this development as a positive sign for the country's economic stability. The easing of inflation could influence future monetary policy decisions by the central bank, potentially impacting interest rates and economic growth. This trend is being closely monitored by economists and financial analysts, as it may signal a shift in the economic landscape of South Africa.

data.title: Polygon fees exceed Ethereum driven by Polymarket surge
data.description:
Polygon collected $407,100 in transaction fees on 14 February 2026, surpassing Ethereum's $211,700. Polymarket activity contributed 66. 7% of Polygon's fee revenue.
source: https://coinpaprika.com/news/polygon-fees-exceed-ethereum-driven-polymarket-surge

data.title: Ethereum Faces Challenges Amid Shifts in Scaling and Privacy Features
data.description: Ethereum is undergoing a significant transition as its traditional Layer 2 scaling narrative evolves to incorporate zero-knowledge privacy features at the base layer. According to NS3.AI, this shift is affecting investor valuation models and presents challenges in attracting substantial institutional capital, which requires enhanced privacy for strategic operations. Furthermore, developments in options markets related to spot ETFs are impacting Ethereum's price dynamics, indicating a complex environment for its valuation and adoption.
